summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a legendary 1969 show featuring an epic Dark Star, stellar St. Stephen and a blazing The Eleven.
review: Fans rave about this February‑27 1969 Fillmore West performance, calling the Dark Star the quintessential version and highlighting flawless transitions from Mountains of the Moon into the jam. The recording is praised as crystal‑clear and more open than the Live/Dead mix, making the entire set – especially The Eleven, Lovelight and the Cryptical > O1 > Cryptical suite – a must‑hear for any Deadhead.

ai_rating.rationale: Over thirty detailed 5‑star reviews praise both the musical performance and audio quality; no significant criticisms were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the mix as open, clear and even superior to the Live/Dead master; one note mentions Bobby (drums) being higher in the mix but overall balance is praised.

standout_songs: Mountains of the Moon → Dark Star transition
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is lauded for its seamless flow – Mountains of the Moon feeds into a transcendent Dark Star, followed by a powerful St. Stephen/William Tell, and a second‑half suite that rides through Cryptical, The Other One, The Eleven and Lovelight with superb pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring leads, cosmic solos on Dark Star and Lovelight, and “coasters of red‑white‑blue light” during his guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “throwing down the thunder” on bass, anchoring the improvisations.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for fluid right‑hand fills and rhythmic support; his promise to "bring out the monkey" is remembered.
band_member_comments.Keys: Organ interplay called stellar, especially with Jerry; overall keyboard work deemed excellent throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bobby’s drumming highlighted – higher in the mix than on Live/Dead but powerful; drum breaks praised.
